# atomwrite

> Atomic file operations for LLM agents ‚Äî one CLI, zero corruption

atomwrite is a Rust CLI that provides 41 subcommands for file manipulation with atomic write guarantees. Every write uses tempfile + fsync + rename + directory fsync. Every response is NDJSON with BLAKE3 checksums. Built for LLM agents that need reliable, structured file operations without corruption risk.

**Latest release: v0.1.35** ‚Äî Residual A-* closed: large overwrite **default-deny** (`--ack-overwrite`); `watch` emits `watch_summary` even with 0 events; `semantic-merge` conflict markers default ON; `delete --plan` (not `--confirm`/`--yes`); watch idle 500 ms; suite `cli_e2e_v0135` green. Pin `^0.1.35`. 41 subcommands; no telemetry; CLI+XDG config only. See `CHANGELOG.md` and gaps.md ¬ß20.

**Previous:** v0.1.34 docs-complete one-shot fuzzy;  v0.1.33 runtime hang fix + one-pass fuzzy; v0.1.32 docs.rs `doc_cfg` / multi-target; v0.1.30 agent surface (fuzzy mandatory, `match_count`/`indent_adjusted`, `best_candidate`, recipe bak skip, `cli_v0130_agent_contract`). See `CHANGELOG.md`.

## 41 Subcommands

### Core I/O (7)
- `read` ‚Äî read files with metadata, checksum, optional content. `--mode raw|envelope` selects byte-stream vs NDJSON envelope
- `write` ‚Äî create or overwrite files atomically via stdin; large targets require `--ack-overwrite` (default-deny). `--preserve-timestamps` keeps source mtime
- `edit` ‚Äî surgically edit by line number, text marker, or exact/fuzzy match; multi-pair `--old`/`--new` with per-pair fuzzy cascade, `pair_results` and `--partial` (v0.1.15)
- `delete` ‚Äî delete files with optional backup; plan-only via `--plan` (`--confirm`/`--yes` rejected)
- `copy` ‚Äî copy files with checksum verification
- `move` ‚Äî move or rename files atomically (EXDEV copy-fallback)
- `apply` ‚Äî apply patches from stdin (unified diff, search/replace, full, markdown)

### Search and replace (3)
- `search` ‚Äî search file contents in parallel (ripgrep engine). `--no-begin-end` disables anchor emission
- `replace` ‚Äî replace text across files with atomic writes; `--fuzzy auto|aggressive|off` (`off` = exact-only, G-010; default `auto`) and `--fuzzy-threshold`; **one-pass** multi-apply on original content (never re-scans inserted text); default max applies = **1**; if replacement embeds pattern ‚Üí force 1 apply; reports `strategy`/`similarity`/`match_count`/`indent_adjusted`; match errors may include `best_candidate`
- `transform` ‚Äî AST refactoring via ast-grep (306 languages)

### Inspection (9)
- `hash` ‚Äî calculate BLAKE3 checksums
- `count` ‚Äî count lines, files by extension. `--by-size` lists largest files
- `diff` ‚Äî compare two files (unified, stat, or changes)
- `list` ‚Äî list files in a directory tree
- `extract` ‚Äî extract fields from NDJSON input via pipe
- `scope` ‚Äî grammatical scoping (delete all comments, etc.). `--lang rust` alias accepted
- `regex` ‚Äî generate regex from examples
- `calc` ‚Äî math and unit conversions
- `completions` ‚Äî generate shell completions (bash, zsh, fish, elvish, powershell)

### Backup and recovery (3)
- `backup` ‚Äî create timestamped backups with BLAKE3 checksums
- `rollback` ‚Äî restore from a previous backup
- `batch` ‚Äî NDJSON-driven batch operations (transactional)

### Batch helpers (2)
- `edit-loop` ‚Äî N edits in 1 invocation via JSON array or NDJSON stdin
- `prune-backups` ‚Äî manual cleanup of legacy `.bak.*` files with `--max-age-secs`, `--max-count`

### WAL diagnostics (2)
- `wal-stats` ‚Äî snapshot of WAL journal state (counts, ages, sizes)
- `wal-heal` ‚Äî remove orphan terminal journals older than threshold

### Verification (1)
- `verify <PATH> <HASH>` ‚Äî verify BLAKE3 checksum of a file (delegates to `hash --verify`)

### Structured config editors (v14 Tier 3, v0.1.12) (4)
- `set <PATH> <KEY_PATH> <VALUE>` ‚Äî write a value at a dotted path in a TOML or JSON file. Preserves comments and key order via `toml_edit`. Auto-coerces int/bool/float/string.
- `get <PATH> <KEY_PATH>` ‚Äî read a value at a dotted path. NDJSON: `{"type":"get","key_path","value","found","format"}`.
- `del <PATH> <KEY_PATH>` ‚Äî remove a key. `--force-missing` flag treats missing keys as a no-op success.
- `case <PATHS...> --subvert OLD NEW --to <style>` ‚Äî rename identifiers across multiple files via `heck`. Styles: `snake`, `camel`, `pascal`, `kebab`, `screaming-snake`.

### AST tools (v14 Tier 3 + G72, v0.1.12, via tree-sitter-language-pack) (2)
- `query <PATH> [--kinds|--query <KIND>|-Q <KIND>|--tree] [--positions]` ‚Äî walk a tree-sitter AST and emit nodes as NDJSON. 305 languages supported.
- `outline <PATH> [--kind <KIND>] [--positions]` ‚Äî extract high-level structure (functions, classes, structs, enums, traits, modules) as NDJSON.

Plus the G72 syntax check on `write --syntax-check` (24 languages, exit 88).

### Agent surface and workflows (v0.1.29+, residual v0.1.30) (8)
- `recipe` ‚Äî list/run versioned multi-step pipelines (e.g. search‚Üíreplace‚Üíhash)
- `sparse` ‚Äî budgeted monorepo list/read/outline (outline emits real AST outline_item) (`sparse list --max-files N`)
- `semantic-merge` ‚Äî line-based three-way merge for multi-agent conflicts (not AST)
- `agent-surface` ‚Äî clap-derived tool manifesto (CLI only; MCP is FORBIDDEN)
- `watch` ‚Äî filesystem events with debounce/checksum/gitignore; always ends with `watch_summary` NDJSON (feature `watch`)
- `codemod` ‚Äî multi-rule AST campaign with `by_rule_id` summary
- `semantic-search` ‚Äî offline token ranking / inverted index (feature `semantic`; not OpenRouter embeddings)
- `stat` ‚Äî alias of `read --stat`

### Write durability (v0.1.29)
- `write --durability full|fast|auto` ‚Äî fsync trade-offs; NDJSON `platform.durability`, `platform.rename_method`, `platform.backup_method`
- Linux uses `renameat2` when available, falls back to rename
- macOS uses `F_FULLFSYNC` under durability `full`

### Cargo features (v0.1.29)
- Slim: `cargo build --release --no-default-features --features core`
- Full: `cargo build --release --features full`
- Defaults: `core` + `ast` + `lang-rust` + `lang-ts` + `lang-py`

## v0.1.20 Highlights ‚Äî Intention Guards

- **5 intention-guard flags** form a new safety layer that intercepts destructive mutations before they touch the disk:
  - `--require-backup <N>` ‚Äî refuse the operation when fewer than `N` retained backups exist for the target
  - `--confirm` ‚Äî historical interactive confirm (v0.1.20); **superseded in v0.1.35** by one-shot `--ack-overwrite` / reject `delete --confirm`
  - `--auto-rotate <N>` ‚Äî automatically rotate the backup ring down to `N` entries after a successful write
  - `--risk-threshold <LOW|MEDIUM|HIGH>` ‚Äî block operations whose classified risk meets or exceeds the threshold
  - `--locale <en|pt-BR>` ‚Äî renamed from `--lang` to avoid clashing with the language selector used by `scope` and `transform` (G121+)
- **`count --by-size`** ‚Äî list the largest files in the tree with sizes and line counts
- **`read --mode raw|envelope`** ‚Äî select between byte-stream output and structured NDJSON envelope
- **`search --no-begin-end`** ‚Äî disable the implicit `^` and `$` anchor decoration in regex output
- **`write --preserve-timestamps`** ‚Äî keep the source file mtime when overwriting
- **542 tests passing** in 47 integration suites, 0 failures, 3 Windows cross-compile targets green
- **19 ADRs** in `docs/decisions/` (0019-0037): v0.1.20 adds 0031 (exit code canonicalization), 0032 (intention guards as new layer), 0033-0037 covering scope/lang alias, locale rename, by-size count, read mode, no-begin-end

## v0.1.20 Migration From v0.1.19

- `--lang` on global config commands is renamed to `--locale` to disambiguate from the tree-sitter `--lang` used by `scope` and `transform`. Use `sd` or `ruplacer` to update existing scripts:
  - `rg -l -- '--lang\b' .` then `ruplacer --subvert --lang --locale` (or `fd -e sh -e md -x sd -- '--lang\b' '--locale' {}`)
- Intention guards are OPT-IN. Default behavior is unchanged
- `count --by-size` is ADDITIVE. Default `count` output unchanged
- `read --mode raw` is ADDITIVE. Default envelope output unchanged
- See `docs/MIGRATION.md` for the full upgrade guide

## Primary Documentation

- [README](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/README.md): Full documentation with all 41 subcommands and examples
- [HOW_TO_USE](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/docs/HOW_TO_USE.md): Quickstart guide
- [COOKBOOK](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/docs/COOKBOOK.md): Recipes for common tasks
- [AGENTS.md](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/docs/AGENTS.md): Agent discovery file with output contract and token budget
- [TESTING](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/docs/TESTING.md): 542 tests across 47 test suites
- [MIGRATION](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/docs/MIGRATION.md): Migration guide between versions
- [INSTALL](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/docs/INSTALL.md): Platform-specific install instructions
- [CROSS_PLATFORM](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/docs/CROSS_PLATFORM.md): macOS, Linux, Windows quirks
- [CHANGELOG](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md): Release history
- [SECURITY](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/SECURITY.md): Vulnerability reporting
- [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md): Contributor onboarding, local DoD, PR and release process
- [CODE_OF_CONDUCT](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md): Contributor Covenant 2.1 community standards
- [docs/decisions/](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/docs/decisions/): 29 ADRs (0019-0047, v0.1.12 through v0.1.24 architectural decisions)
- [docs/schemas/](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite/blob/main/docs/schemas/): 38 stable JSON Schema contracts
- [Crates.io](https://crates.io/crates/atomwrite): Package registry
- [Repository](https://github.com/danilo-aguiar-br/atomwrite): Source code
